subject: Computer Networking
title: Net Neutrality Explained
description: A micro-lesson that makes Net Neutrality Explained usable.

hook.text: Should your ISP decide which websites load fast and which load slow? That's the net neutrality debate.

buildup.text: Net neutrality says all internet traffic should be treated equally. No fast lanes for those who pay more.

discovery.text: Without it, ISPs could slow down competitors or charge websites for priority access to customers.

twist.text: Some argue ISPs need flexibility to manage congestion. Others say that's a slippery slope to gatekeeping.

climax.text: Rules vary by country and change with each administration. The battle over net neutrality is far from settled.

punchline.text: Net neutrality asks: who controls the speed of the internet?

quiz.question: What does net neutrality require?
options: ISPs treat all internet traffic equally regardless of source
All websites must be free to access
